title: Loom per-project configuration (loom.config.json)
description: Lives at the target repo root. Lets the human state preferences once; agents read it every session. Created on the first /loom plan run from templates/loom.config.json.

loom_version.description: Loom version this config was written against.

domain_id.description: Primary exact domain selected by loom_domain; scopes owner-memory selection. Use unclassified only until intake resolves it.

domain_ids.description: Ordered matched domains from loom_domain; domain_id is the primary.

autonomy.description: Operative autonomy level per loom/core/autonomy.md. Session words override this; this overrides the default.

pack_path.description: Where the planning pack lives, relative to repo root. Must be gitignored if the repo is public (privacy rule 3).

min_reversibility.description: Agent may auto-decide only choices at or above this reversibility.

spend_limit.description: Maximum spend (in the requester's currency) the agent may commit without asking. 0 = any real money is a human matter.

hard_stops_extra.description: Project-specific never-delegable items, added to the standard hard-stop list (e.g. "anything touching MQL5/Experts/").

routing_map.description: Current capability-class -> model mapping for this project (loom/execution/routing.md). Keys are the five classes.

language.description: Primary human language / locale for deliverables (e.g. "en", "fa", "th"). Localization decisions still follow loom/adaptation/.

ask_me_first.description: Verbatim topics the requester wants surfaced before any action, regardless of autonomy level.

use_profile.description: false = this project runs fully stateless: no ~/.loom reads/writes, no outbox entries (loom/core/user-memory.md rule 7). CLI --no-profile does the same per run.
